About Oideya

A pre-war machiya
in downtown Osaka.

Located in Yodogawa-ku, Osaka, a short walk from Kanzakigawa Station. A pre-war machiya carefully restored with modern comforts — yours entirely as a private whole-house rental.

The ground-floor living room pairs exposed dark timber beams with Bauhaus prints, a cobalt-blue sofa, and a gallery wall — a refined modern interior. Upstairs, sliding shoji screens and tatami floors create a quietly traditional bedroom. Two entirely different worlds, one roof.

Umeda (Osaka-Umeda Station) is just 5 min by train. Namba, Dotonbori, and Osaka Castle are all within easy reach — whether you want a sightseeing base or a slow, neighbourhood-style stay.

🏆 Booking.com 8.5 · Traveller Review Awards 2026 Winner

2F: Bedrooms (3 double beds + 2 futons) · Balcony

Rooms & Space

The Space

The pre-war bones, reimagined with modern comfort and considered design.

Entrance & Exterior
A warm welcome from a hand-crafted wooden sign and lantern light. The entrance retains the original charm of the machiya.
Living / Dining / Kitchen (1F)
Dark timber beams meet Bauhaus prints. A kotatsu, large TV, and two sofas make it ideal for groups.
Tatami Bedrooms (2F)
Soft light through shoji screens. Double beds on tatami floors — traditional Japan meets modern sleep comfort.
